titleStmt.title: SII 25.94: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 94. (A.R. No. 94 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the Central Shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This record may be assigned to the 12th century on palaeographical groungs.

It records a gift of 300 kalam of paddy from out of the dēvadāṉa villages Pottikkuḍi and Viḷakkamaṅgalam of the deity Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār by Rājēndraśōḻaṉ Kēraḷaṉ alias Nishādharāyan of Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalaināḍu, for the festival of pavitrārōhaṇa to be conducted in the month of Aippigai (Aippaśi) to the god Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

The king Parākrama-pāṇḍya may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Parākrama, who is known to have been a contemporary of Kulōttuṅga I as said earlier. The Vikkiramaṅgalam record of Parākrama Pāṇḍya, who is also identified with the above Māṟavarmaṉ, refers to a gift of the 40th year of Kulōttuṅga (See A.R.Ep., 1927, para II, para 39).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvanac cakkaravattikaḷ śrīparākkirama pāṇṭiyadevaṟku yā
ṇṭu 10 1vatu keraḷaciṅkavaḷa nāṭṭu brahmateyam tirupputtūr tiruttaḷiyāṇ
ṭāṟku puṟamalaimanāṭṭup poṉṉamarāpati Irājentra coḻan keraḷanāṉa niṣatarā
yan tiruttaḷiyāṇṭār tevatāṉam keraḷaciṅkavaḷa nāṭṭu pottikkuṭiyilum viḷa
kkamaṅkalattilum tiṉakku kkum paricaṭṭattukkum tantu varukiṟa nellu muṉ
nūṟṟuk kalamum Ittevaṟku Ā Aiyppikaiy māsattu pavitrāroha
ṇam paṇṇa candrādityavat Eṉ taṉmamākac ceytuk kuṭutteṉ tiruttaḷiyāṇṭāṟku I
rājentiracoḻaṉ keraḷaṉāṉa niṣatarāyaṉeṉ Itu panmāheśvara Irakṣaiḥ
Digital edition of SII 25.94 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
